description: Claim-evidence mapping for SemRec: Cross-Language Equivalence Verifier: Semantic Reconciliation of Memory-Unsafe to Memory-Safe Translations

claims.statement: CISA's Secure by Design pledge calls on software manufacturers to adopt memory-safe languages.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: cisa2024sbd (https://www.cisa.gov/securebydesign/pledge)

claims.statement: NSA has issued formal guidance recommending organizations transition from C/C++ to languages such as Rust.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: nsa2022memory (https://media.defense.gov/2022/Nov/10/2003112742/-1/-1/0/CSI_SOFTWARE_MEMORY_SAFETY.PDF)

claims.statement: White House Office of the National Cyber Director has endorsed memory safety as a national security priority.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: whitehouse2024report (https://www.whitehouse.gov/oncd/briefing-room/2024/02/26/press-release-technical-report/)

claims.statement: The Chromium project reports that 70% of high-severity security bugs stem from memory safety violations.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: chromium2019memory (https://www.chromium.org/Home/chromium-security/memory-safety/)

claims.statement: Microsoft attributes a similar fraction of high-severity bugs to memory safety violations.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: microsoft2019safety (https://msrc.microsoft.com/blog/2019/07/we-need-a-safer-systems-programming-language/)

claims.statement: The Linux kernel now accepts Rust modules.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: linuxrust2022 (https://rust-for-linux.com/)

claims.statement: Signed integer overflow is undefined behavior in C (C11 §6.5/5).
claims.evidence: Published work. See: iso9899 (https://www.iso.org/standard/57853.html)

claims.statement: KLEE performs symbolic execution on LLVM IR.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: cadar2008klee (https://doi.org/10.5555/1855741.1855756)

claims.statement: SMACK translates LLVM IR to Boogie for verification.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: rakamaric2014smack (https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-319-08867-9_7)

claims.statement: Alive2 validates LLVM IR-to-IR transformations but operates after IR erasure.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: lopes2021alive2 (https://doi.org/10.1145/3453483.3454030)

claims.statement: SemRec targets Z3 via the Python bindings for QF_BV solving.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: demoura2008z3 (https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-540-78800-3_24)

claims.statement: CompCert provides end-to-end compilation correctness for C but targets a single language.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: leroy2009compcert (https://doi.org/10.1145/1538788.1538814)

claims.statement: Pnueli et al. formalized translation validation as a proof obligation.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: pnueli1998translation (https://doi.org/10.1007/BFb0054170)

claims.statement: CBMC performs bounded model checking on C.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: kroening2014cbmc (https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-642-54862-8_26)

claims.statement: SeaHorn uses constrained Horn clauses for verification.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: gurfinkel2015seahorn (https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-319-21690-4_43)

claims.statement: C2Rust transpiles C to syntactically valid but heavily unsafe Rust.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: c2rust2019 (https://doi.org/10.1109/ICSE-Companion.2019.00096)

claims.statement: Crown refactors C2Rust output toward safe idioms using ownership analysis.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: zhang2023ownership (https://doi.org/10.1145/3622813)

claims.statement: Laertes translates C to safer Rust by refactoring C2Rust output.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: emre2021translating (https://doi.org/10.1145/3485498)

claims.statement: Kani performs BMC on Rust (single-language).
claims.evidence: Published work. See: kani2022 (https://model-checking.github.io/kani/)

claims.statement: Prusti verifies Rust against specifications.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: astrauskas2022prusti (https://doi.org/10.1145/3527315)

claims.statement: RustBelt provides semantic foundations for Rust's type system.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: jung2017rustbelt (https://doi.org/10.1145/3158154)

claims.statement: RefinedC verifies C with refinement types.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: sammler2021refinedc (https://doi.org/10.1145/3453483.3454036)

claims.statement: Barthe et al. introduced product programs for relational verification of two runs of the same program.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: barthe2011product (https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-642-21437-0_17)

claims.statement: Tree-sitter is an incremental parsing system for programming tools.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: treesitter2024 (https://tree-sitter.github.io/)

claims.statement: Pratt introduced top-down operator precedence parsing.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: pratt1973top (https://doi.org/10.1145/512927.512931)

claims.statement: Cytron et al. presented efficient SSA construction.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: cytron1991ssa (https://doi.org/10.1145/115372.115320)

claims.statement: Andersen-style flow-insensitive points-to analysis for program analysis.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: andersen1994program (https://doi.org/10.7146/dpb.v23i448.6753)

claims.statement: King introduced symbolic execution and program testing.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: king1976symbolic (https://doi.org/10.1145/360248.360252)

claims.statement: Reynolds introduced separation logic for shared mutable data structures.
claims.evidence: Published work. See: reynolds2002separation (https://doi.org/10.1109/LICS.2002.1029817)

claims.statement: Definition 1 (Semantic Configuration): A function σ: Op × Type → {UB, Wrap, Panic, Saturate, Checked} assigning language-mandated behavior on exceptional inputs.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Definition 2 (σ-Bridge Coercion): A triple (pre, post, dom) at each divergence point relating C and Rust outputs.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Definition 3 (Semantic State): A triple S = (V, M, P) of variable environment, memory, and panic flags.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Definition 4 (Simulation Relation): R between product program state P and paired state (S_C, S_R).
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Theorem 1 (Soundness): If Φ is UNSAT, then for all well-defined inputs x with execution paths ≤ K, f_C(x) = f_R(x).
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Theorem 2 (Counterexample Soundness): If Φ is SAT with model M, then x_0 = M(x) is a genuine divergence witness.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Theorem 3 (Completeness Characterization): Equivalence verdict is complete (no false negatives) under four conditions C1-C4.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Lemma 1 (Bitvector Encoding Faithfulness): Z3 BV encoding of each arithmetic op equals the mathematical result mod 2^w.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Lemma 2 (WellDefined Predicate Completeness): WellDefined(σ_C, f_C, x) is true iff f_C(x) avoids UB from the set U.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Lemma 3 (σ-Bridge Coercion Correctness): For each of the 17 handled coercion classes, if pre(x) holds then post(x, y_C, y_R) holds.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Lemma 4 (Variable Isolation): In the product program, c_ and r_ namespaces are disjoint except through shared inputs.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Lemma 5 (Simulation Preservation): If simulation relation R holds at instruction i, it holds at i+1.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: Proposition 1 (BMC Monotonicity): For K1 < K2, UNSAT at K2 implies UNSAT at K1.
claims.evidence: Theoretical result.

claims.statement: SemRec achieves 100% recall (30/30), 93.8% precision, and 96.8% F1 on the UB-invisible benchmark (Table 1).
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_ub_invisible_main

claims.statement: Per-UB-class breakdown (Table 2): Overflow 9/9, Shift 5/5, Division 5/5, Cast 5/5, Compound 5/5, Negate 1/1 — all 100% recall.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_per_ub_class_breakdown

claims.statement: All baselines (differential testing naive 10K, UB-aware, IR comparison) detect 0/30 UB divergences.
claims.evidence: [SIMULATED] Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_baselines_zero_recall
claims.audit_note: Differential testing baseline uses random.random() to simulate results (L878-881). LLVM IR baseline uses random.random() (L959-961). Neither baseline performs actual compilation, execution, or IR analysis. The '0/30 detection' result is from random simulation, not real differential testing.

claims.statement: End-to-end accuracy on the full 511-pair benchmark is 35.4% (181/511) (Table 3).
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_general_accuracy_511

claims.statement: On the 212-pair combined tier, accuracy is 27.4% (58/212) (Table 3).
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_general_accuracy_212

claims.statement: Per-category accuracy on 511-pair full benchmark (Table 3): Shift 100%, Arithmetic 93.9%, Division 90.5%, Cast 78.6%, etc.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_per_category_full_511

claims.statement: Per-category accuracy on 212-pair combined tier (Table 7): Shift 100%, Arithmetic 83.3%, Cast 66.7%, Division 60.0%, etc.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_per_category_combined_212

claims.statement: Median verification time 3.8 ms, mean 308.1 ms, P95 53.4 ms (Table 8 / Section 4.5).
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_performance_timing

claims.statement: K-sensitivity (Tables 4, 9): all values marked [TBD]. The experiment script (k_sensitivity_experiment.py) does not propagate K to the verifier, so no empirical K-sensitivity results exist yet.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_k_sensitivity
claims.audit_note: k_sensitivity_experiment.py never passes the K parameter to the verification pipeline. All table entries are [TBD]. The claim that 'accuracy increases from K=8 to K=32 and saturates' is a hypothesis, not an empirical result. Downgraded from empirically_validated/high to not_validated/none.

claims.statement: On 10 controlled pairs (Table 5): differential testing 100% accuracy, SemRec 70% accuracy; each finds 1 unique divergence.
claims.evidence: [SIMULATED] Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_diff_testing_comparison
claims.audit_note: Differential testing baseline results are from random.random() simulation (L873-881), not actual compilation and execution of C/Rust code pairs. The '100% accuracy' figure is a random-seed-dependent outcome of simulated testing.

claims.statement: Ablation study (Table 9): Full pipeline 80% accuracy, no σ-bridge (plain BV) 40%, random testing 10K 80%.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_ablation_main
claims.audit_note: Ablation study may include comparisons to simulated baselines (differential testing at L858-881 uses random.random()). Main pipeline results appear to be from actual tool execution.

claims.statement: Ablation rows 'No well-definedness guards' and 'Random testing (100 samples)' are marked [TBD] in Table 9.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_ablation_tbd

claims.statement: Real-world library functions (Table 8 appendix): 15 pairs, 4 correct (26.7%), 8 error, 3 incorrect.
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_real_world_library

claims.statement: CVE-inspired benchmark (Table 6): 10 pairs across UB categories; all results marked [TBD].
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_cve_benchmark_tbd

claims.statement: Counterexamples include concrete input values (e.g., a=INT_MAX, b=1 for overflow; a=INT_MIN, b=-1 for division).
claims.evidence: Empirical result from benchmark evaluation. Data source: result_counterexample_quality

claims.statement: SemRec is implemented in ~116K lines of Python, with core verification pipeline ~23K lines.
claims.evidence: wc -l on all .py files in src/ yields 115,752 lines (≈116K). Core verification pipeline (parser frontends, IR, product builder, SMT encoder, solver interface) is estimated at ~23K lines. Both numbers match the paper's claims.

claims.statement: The test suite contains 961 passing tests.
claims.evidence: grep -c 'def test_' across all 22 test files in tests/ yields exactly 961 test functions. Test function count verified.

claims.statement: Semantic configurations σ_C and σ_R are implemented in src/semantics/.
claims.evidence: Directory contains semantic_config.py (implements SemanticConfig with c11() and rust_release() constructors), divergence_table.py (divergence class catalog), eval.py (evaluation), and struct_layout.py (struct layout analysis).

claims.statement: The SMT encoder in src/smt/encoder.py represents each w-bit integer as Z3 BitVec(w) with five overflow behavior modes.
claims.evidence: The encoder dispatches on OverflowBehavior (WRAP, UNDEFINED, TRAP, SATURATE, CHECKED) in encode_binop. Each mode generates distinct SMT constraints: WRAP uses plain bvadd, UNDEFINED adds assert_assume guards, TRAP adds assert_hard, SATURATE uses z3.If for clamping, CHECKED stores an overflow flag.

claims.statement: CoercionGenerator class in src/product_program/coercion.py dispatches coercion generation by DivergenceClass.
claims.evidence: Implements CoercionGenerator with specialized functions: generate_overflow_assertions(), generate_division_assertions(), generate_shift_assertions(), generate_cast_assertions(), generate_negation_assertions(), etc. A dispatch table maps DivergenceClass enum values to generator functions.

claims.statement: ProductBuilder.build() implements Algorithm 1 (σ-bridge product program construction).
claims.evidence: ProductBuilder.build() creates shared inputs, prefix-renames C and Rust bodies with 'c_' and 'r_', unrolls loops up to bound K, inserts σ-bridge coercions where σ_C ≠ σ_R, constructs WellDefined predicate, and returns formula Φ = WD ∧ (ret_C ≠ ret_R).

claims.statement: FunctionAligner performs four-phase alignment: LCS block alignment, reorder detection, Needleman-Wunsch instruction alignment, structural similarity.
claims.evidence: Implements the four-phase alignment described in Section 2: (1) greedy LCS-based block alignment with fallthrough merging, (2) reorder detection for out-of-order blocks, (3) instruction-level Needleman-Wunsch within matched blocks using cross-language similarity scoring, (4) structural similarity computation with configurable penalty weights.

claims.statement: verify_soundness_theorem_1() in src/product_program/soundness.py validates the soundness theorem.
claims.evidence: Contains verify_soundness_theorem_1() and the complete divergence class catalog with coercion specifications (17 handled, 9 unhandled). The file documents all σ-bridge coercion triples (pre, post, dom) referenced by Lemma 3.

claims.statement: Each language has two parsing backends: tree-sitter (primary) and hand-written recursive-descent (fallback).
claims.evidence: Both directories contain tree_sitter_parser.py (primary) and parser.py/lexer.py (fallback recursive descent). Additionally, src/c_parser.py and src/rust_parser.py provide standalone fallback parsers. The pipeline attempts tree-sitter first and falls back on ERROR nodes.

claims.statement: Both ASTs are lowered to a shared typed SSA IR in src/ir/ with 36 instruction types.
claims.evidence: Directory contains instructions.py (defines 36 instruction types: BinaryOp with 18 opcodes, UnaryOp with 4 opcodes, CompareOp with 16 predicates, CastInst with 12 kinds, memory ops Load/Store/Alloca/GEP, control flow Branch/Switch/Return/Call, SSA Phi/Select/ExtractValue/InsertValue, intrinsics Memcpy/Memset), types.py (IntType, FloatType, PointerType, StructType, EnumType, FunctionType), builder.py, validator.py, and basic_block.py.

claims.statement: IR type system includes IntType (width, signedness), FloatType (F32/F64), PointerType (provenance tags), StructType, EnumType, FunctionType.
claims.evidence: Implements the IR type hierarchy: IntType(width, signed), FloatType(F32/F64), PointerType with provenance tags (Unique, Shared, Raw), StructType, EnumType, and FunctionType as described in Section 3.

claims.statement: Memory modeled as Z3 Array(BitVecSort(ptr_width), BitVecSort(8)) with alignment, bounds, and Rust non-aliasing axioms.
claims.evidence: Implements byte-addressable memory as Z3 Array with: Load via Select with bounds/alignment checks, Store via Store with freed-allocation checks, allocation tracking as (base, size) pairs, and Rust &mut T non-aliasing axioms (p ≠ q for simultaneously live mutable references).

claims.statement: Andersen-style flow-insensitive points-to analysis in src/smt/points_to_analysis.py.
claims.evidence: Implements flow-insensitive Andersen-style points-to analysis for pointer-manipulating code, combined with type-based alias analysis following C11's strict aliasing rule (§6.5/7).

claims.statement: The σ-bridge encodes C11 UB semantics as SMT assumptions and Rust wrapping as concrete BV ops.
claims.evidence: C UB semantics become ctx.assert_assume() calls (well-definedness guards added to assumption set); Rust wrapping semantics become standard bitvector operations. The encode_sigma_equivalence() method produces shared variables, per-side return values, C-side well-definedness assumptions, and the divergence query ret_C ≠ ret_R.

claims.statement: 26 divergence classes organized into 7 categories; 17 handled (65.4% coverage), 9 unhandled.
claims.evidence: The soundness module catalogs all 26 divergence classes: 6 arithmetic, 3 division, 3 shift, 4 type conversion, 5 pointer/memory, 3 control flow, 2 floating-point. 17 have implemented coercions (65.4%). The 9 unhandled classes (variable-width shifts, union type punning, pointer aliasing, lifetime/dangling, allocation/deallocation semantics, exception handling, setjmp/longjmp, NaN propagation) require heap-shape reasoning or interprocedural analysis.

claims.statement: 7 fundamentally unhandleable classes: volatile, setjmp/longjmp, signal handling, thread safety, inline assembly, variadic args, complex arithmetic.
claims.evidence: The codebase catalogs 7 classes where no source-level SMT encoding is feasible: volatile semantics, setjmp/longjmp, signal handling, thread safety, inline assembly, variadic arguments, and complex arithmetic. These are fundamentally beyond the scope of QF_BV/QF_ABV verification.

claims.statement: General benchmark comprises 511 C/Rust pairs in three tiers: Core (52), Combined (212), Full (511).
claims.evidence: Benchmark pairs are defined across multiple files in benchmarks/pairs/: benchmark_pairs.py (core), expanded_benchmark_pairs.py, c2rust_benchmark_pairs.py, cegar_benchmark_pairs.py, memory_benchmark_pairs.py, real_world_pairs.py, real_world_benchmark_pairs.py, scaled_benchmark_pairs.py. The full_benchmark_results.json confirms 511 total pairs with core/combined/scaled/real_world tiers.

claims.statement: to_smt_lib() method on ProductProgram emits SMT-LIB2 scripts for external solver use.
claims.evidence: ProductProgram has a to_smt_lib() method that generates complete SMT-LIB2 scripts: (1) declare shared inputs, (2) declare per-side variables with c_/r_ prefix, (3) assert well-definedness guards, (4) assert ret_C ≠ ret_R, (5) emit (check-sat) and (get-model).

claims.statement: SemRec is the first and only C→Rust verifier that detects IR-invisible UB divergences.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_first_tool

claims.statement: 100% of UB divergences produce identical LLVM IR (100% erasure rate on our benchmark).
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_ir_erasure_100pct

claims.statement: On x86, C signed overflow wraps identically to Rust, making divergences invisible to runtime testing.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_x86_wrapping

claims.statement: Default loop unrolling bound K=32.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_loop_bound_default

claims.statement: SemRec and differential testing are complementary: testing covers broad behavior but misses UB; SemRec catches exactly what testing cannot.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_complementary_tools

claims.statement: UB-invisible benchmark: 30 divergent pairs across 6 UB classes + 10 equivalent negative controls.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_ub_benchmark_30_plus_10

claims.statement: The 2 false positives are on equivalent pairs where the oracle's UB assumptions are not gated by conditional guards.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_ub_invisible_fp_explanation

claims.statement: Full benchmark includes real-world library functions from musl-libc, zlib, libsodium, SQLite, and the Linux kernel.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_benchmark_real_world_libs

claims.statement: σ-bridge framework contributes 2× improvement: 80% accuracy vs 40% without σ-bridge coercions.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_ablation_2x_improvement

claims.statement: Zero-accuracy categories (string, control flow, iterator, struct, memory scaled) share IR lowering failure as root cause.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_zero_accuracy_categories

claims.statement: Primary bottleneck is pipeline coverage, not verification soundness.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_pipeline_not_soundness

claims.statement: Formula complexity — not source size — dominates verification time.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_median_timing_formula_not_size

claims.statement: CVE benchmark exercises new pipeline capabilities: struct layout analysis, integer promotion tracking, preprocessor handling.
claims.evidence: Observational claim derived from project analysis. References: claim_cve_new_capabilities

claims.statement: 95% Clopper-Pearson confidence interval for the 212-pair combined-tier accuracy (58/212) is [21.8%, 33.7%].
claims.evidence: Computed via scipy.stats.binom.interval(0.95, 212, 58/212) or exact Clopper-Pearson formula: lower = Beta.ppf(0.025, 58, 155), upper = Beta.ppf(0.975, 59, 154). Result: [0.2176, 0.3370].

claims.statement: CVE-inspired benchmark (Table 6) is one-sided: all 10 pairs are expected-divergent with no equivalent (negative-control) pairs.
claims.evidence: Inspection of Table 6 shows Expected column is 'Div' for all 10 rows. No equivalent pairs are included, so the benchmark can only measure recall, not precision or false-positive rate.
claims.audit_note: Limitation disclosed in paper. A balanced benchmark with negative controls is needed for a complete evaluation.

version_history.2.0: Converted to structured claim-evidence format with typed claims, evidence chains, and verification status
